name: Quest Marker
description: An objective-state projection that maps quest, mission, or task targets into world, map, compass, or screen-space markers with priority and visibility rules.
design_notes: Quest markers guide attention, but they can also flatten exploration or leak spoilers. The design should decide which objective_id owns the marker, whether markers show exact positions or search areas, how fog/discovery gating works, how distance and verticality are localized, and whether markers are shared, personal, disabled, or filtered.
design_purpose: Help players locate active objectives without searching menus repeatedly.
Communicate objective distance, direction, and completion state.
Reduce navigation friction while preserving discovery where desired.
player_fantasy: The player feels oriented toward their chosen goal without losing track of the world.

parameters.name: objective_id
parameters.description: Quest objective, mission step, or task state that owns marker visibility and completion.

parameters.name: marker_target_id
parameters.description: World entity, area, or map location used for projection and stale-target cleanup.

parameters.name: marker_screen_edge_padding
parameters.description: Padding used when clamping offscreen markers to screen edges.

parameters.name: objective_area_radius
parameters.description: Approximate search area shown instead of exact point.

parameters.name: distance_label_threshold
parameters.description: Distance range where numeric labels should be shown.

parameters.name: marker_priority_weight
parameters.description: Ordering weight when multiple quest markers compete for attention.

edge_cases: Objectives inside caves, buildings, or stacked floors need vertical and pathing cues, not only straight-line direction.
Multiple active quests can flood the HUD unless filtered or prioritized.
Moving objectives and NPCs need marker smoothing without lying about position.
Fog-of-war, discovery, stealth, or spoiler rules may hide the target even though the quest state is active.
Shared multiplayer objectives and personal objectives need different ownership, color, and completion rules.
Markers should handle hidden, failed, completed, inactive, and optional objectives distinctly across save/load.
common_bugs: Marker points to world origin after the quest target despawns.
Straight-line distance sends players toward cliffs or locked doors without context.
Completed markers remain on compass because quest state and marker state update in different systems.
Offscreen clamping overlaps subtitles or critical combat UI.
A marker reveals an undiscovered cave, enemy, or quest branch because fog or spoiler gating was not checked.
Multiplayer clients show different marker completion because objective_id progress is local for one player and shared for another.
balancing_notes: Exact markers reduce friction but can remove exploration and environmental reading.
Area markers preserve mystery while still giving players enough direction to proceed.
accessibility_notes: Use shape, text, and distance labels in addition to color, and provide marker size/opacity settings.
Allow players to reduce marker clutter or pin only one objective at a time.

implementation_notes.unity: Bind markers to objective_id plus target entity, world position, or objective area. Project world-to-screen after camera update, apply fog/spoiler gating, and remove markers when quest state changes to completed, failed, hidden, or unloaded.
implementation_notes.godot: Store marker state as objective_id, target NodePath or area center, and marker mode. Update screen-edge clamps after camera transforms, apply discovery gates, and handle above/below indicators from world height delta.
implementation_notes.unreal: Feed UMG marker widgets from a quest tracker component that owns objective IDs, target actors, and area radii. Use weak actor references, clear widgets when replicated quest state changes, and separate personal versus shared objective state.
implementation_notes.web: Keep quest markers in normalized state keyed by objective_id. Resolve target position each frame, clamp offscreen markers with padding, and hide stale markers when target or quest state is invalid.

relationships.reason: Reputation-aware visibility rules can make quest markers reflect faction access without making reputation gating a baseline marker dependency.
Map projection makes quest markers useful beyond the current camera view or world-space prompt distance.
Generated quests need marker validation so targets are reachable, current, and not leaked before discovery.

example_games.title: The Elder Scrolls V: Skyrim
example_games.note: Uses compass and map quest markers to guide objectives.

example_games.title: Grand Theft Auto IV
example_games.note: Uses mission markers and map destinations to guide city objectives.

example_games.title: Grand Theft Auto V
example_games.note: Uses mission markers and map destinations for open-world activities.

example_games.title: Red Dead Redemption 2
example_games.note: Uses mission and objective markers on the map and HUD to guide activities.

example_games.title: The Legend of Zelda: Breath of the Wild
example_games.note: Uses quest markers and map pins to guide selected objectives and destinations.

example_games.title: The Witcher 3: Wild Hunt
example_games.note: Uses tracked quest markers and minimap routing for objectives.

example_games.title: Fortnite
example_games.note: Uses quest and map markers to guide objectives and points of interest.

example_games.title: Super Mario Odyssey
example_games.note: Uses map markers and destination hints to guide kingdom objectives.

example_games.title: Subnautica
example_games.note: Uses beacon and signal markers to guide exploration targets.

example_games.title: Diablo III
example_games.note: Uses objective markers and map guidance for quests and dungeon goals.

example_games.title: World of Warcraft
example_games.note: Uses quest markers and tracked objectives to guide world and dungeon tasks.

example_games.title: Alien: Isolation
example_games.note: Uses objective guidance and tracker cues to direct survival routes.
